intro: Seidlitzia stocksii, also known as the Stock's saltwort or desert spinach, is a halophytic succulent shrub native to the Middle East and North Africa. The leaves of this plant have been traditionally used to treat various health conditions, including hypertension and inflammation, due to its potential antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties. Bioactive constituents of Seidlitzia stocksii include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and saponins, which contribute to its medicinal properties. The plant is commonly used to prepare herbal teas, infusions, and capsules, which are said to aid in digestion, reduce blood pressure, and alleviate symptoms of anxiety and stress.
